gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Sandwich MEV attack on swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild diverts protocol TitanX to genesisWallet and yields attacker profit
candidate.claimed_root_cause: 10% TWAP-based slippage tolerance in _swapTitanXForTorus and 10% liquiditySlippage in _addLiquidityBuild allow a same-block front-run to move spot price within tolerance, causing fewer Torus purchased and leftover TitanX transferred to genesisWallet instead of being used for LP
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker extracts sandwich profit from protocol interval allocation; protocol TitanX is diverted to genesisWallet rather than added as liquidity

live_preconditions.name: titanXIntervalsBuild[lastTitanXBuildIntervalNumber=53061].amountAllocated > 0 and amountBurned == 0
live_preconditions.observed_value: not directly sampled (live_context only sampled intervals 0 and 1)

live_preconditions.name: TitanX/TORUS pool has sufficient liquidity for attacker front-run to move spot price ~8-10% vs TWAP

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Uses 15-minute TWAP oracle for amountOutMinimum with 10% tolerance; same-block front-run does not move TWAP, so attacker can move spot price up to 10% below TWAP quote without triggering revert
Splits interval allocation into half (swap) and remainder (LP); any unprivileged caller can trigger; no caller restriction beyond nonReentrant and liquidityAddedBuild check

amount0Min/amount1Min set to 90% of desired; leftover tokens after increaseLiquidity are unconditionally transferred to genesisWallet; at manipulated price, leftover TitanX can be material rather than dust
TWAP window is 15 minutes; same-block manipulation does not affect TWAP, confirming oracle provides no protection against within-tolerance spot manipulation

attacker_path.actor: Any unprivileged EOA or contract with sufficient TitanX capital
attacker_inputs: Front-run: ISwapRouter.exactInput TitanX->TORUS on the TitanX/TORUS pool, moving spot price up by ~8-9% vs 15-min TWAP
Back-run: sell acquired TORUS back for TitanX at elevated spot price
call_sequence: 1. Attacker front-run tx: large TitanX->TORUS swap, spot price rises ~8-9% vs TWAP
2. swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild(_deadline): _swapTitanXForTorus(half) executes at manipulated spot, returns fewer TORUS (within 10% tolerance, no revert)
3. _addLiquidityBuild(remainder, fewerTorus): increaseLiquidity absorbs tokens at manipulated ratio; leftover TitanX (amount0 - used0 or amount1 - used1) transferred to genesisWallet
4. Attacker back-run tx: sell TORUS for TitanX at elevated price, capturing sandwich profit
state_after: genesisWallet receives material TitanX leftover (beyond dust)
LP receives less liquidity than expected
Attacker net-positive after gas from sandwich profit

extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Sandwich profit: difference between TitanX spent in front-run and TitanX recovered in back-run after victim tx moves price; attacker is net-positive if manipulation stays within 10% slippage tolerance
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol interval allocation is consumed with less LP efficiency; TitanX that should have been added as liquidity is instead sent to genesisWallet; LP depth is reduced relative to expected
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is entitled to zero protocol TitanX; the sandwich extracts value from the protocol's interval allocation by exploiting the wide slippage tolerance and TWAP oracle's inability to reflect same-block manipulation

setup: Fork Ethereum mainnet at block 25530134 (or later block where liquidityAddedBuild=true and interval 53061 has amountBurned==0)
Fund attacker address with TitanX (e.g., 10x interval amountAllocated) via deal()
Record genesisWallet TitanX balance before attack
Record attacker TitanX balance before attack
transaction_sequence: vm.prank(attacker); ISwapRouter(UNISWAP_V3_ROUTER).exactInput(...) // front-run: TitanX->TORUS, move spot ~8-9% vs TWAP
expected_assertions: uint256 genesisBalanceAfter = IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).balanceOf(genesisWallet); assertGt(genesisBalanceAfter - genesisBalanceBefore, 1e18); // leftover is material, not dust
int256 attackerNet = int256(IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).balanceOf(attacker)) - int256(attackerBalanceBefore); assertGt(attackerNet, 0); // attacker is net-positive after gas
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If genesisWallet leftover is consistently below 1e18 TitanX across all manipulation sizes within 10% tolerance, or if attacker is net-negative after gas at all realistic pool depths, reject as expected-behavior dust rounding
